title: Treasury Reports $18M Uncommitted Cash Due to IBM MQ Reconciliation Failure
content: Treasury operations has flagged an $18 million reconciliation discrepancy caused by a 6-hour IBM MQ message queue backlog during end-of-day settlement processing. Eight trading desk transactions were not acknowledged by the downstream confirmation system, leaving positions unhedged overnight. The root cause is a memory exhaustion issue in the queue manager under high-volume settlement conditions. This creates both financial exposure and potential T+1 settlement rule violation under SEC Rule 204.

title: Palo Alto Firewall Renewal Cost Increase 38% — $2.8M Unbudgeted Security Spend
content: The annual renewal for Palo Alto Networks NGFW licenses, threat prevention subscriptions, and Panorama management has come in at $2.8M above the approved budget, representing a 38% increase driven by subscription model changes and expanded coverage requirements post a network segmentation project. Security budget reallocation is required, potentially impacting planned SIEM upgrade investments in Q3.

title: SOX Section 404 Audit Identifies 3 IT General Controls Deficiencies in Trading Systems
content: External auditors have identified three IT General Controls (ITGC) deficiencies in the equity trading platform during the SOX Section 404 audit: (1) change management controls lack post-implementation review for 22% of trading engine changes, (2) logical access reviews for privileged Bloomberg accounts overdue by 6 weeks, (3) audit log retention for Oracle DB does not meet 7-year requirement under SEC Rule 17a-4. These findings require remediation within 60 days to avoid material weakness designation.
